                    # Create standard cursor configurations for batch workers
          
      152. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    152. 
            

            
              
            

                    #
          
      153. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    153. 
            

            
              
            

                    # Divides total items into roughly equal ranges for each worker.
          
      154. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    154. 
            

            
              
            

                    # Supports optional customization via block.
          
      155. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    155. 
            

            
              
            

                    #
          
      156. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    156. 
            

            
              
            

                    # ## TAS-112: 0-Indexed Cursors (BREAKING CHANGE)
          
      157. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    157. 
            

            
              
            

                    #
          
      158. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    158. 
            

            
              
            

                    # As of TAS-112, cursor indexing is 0-based to match Python, TypeScript, and Rust.
          
      159. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    159. 
            

            
              
            

                    #
          
      160. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    160. 
            

            
              
            

                    # ## Cursor Boundary Math
          
      161. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    161. 
            

            
              
            

                    #
          
      162. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    162. 
            

            
              
            

                    # The method divides total_items into worker_count roughly equal ranges using
          
      163. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    163. 
            

            
              
            

                    # ceiling division to ensure all items are covered:
          
      164. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    164. 
            

            
              
            

                    #
          
      165. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    165. 
            

            
              
            

                    # 1. items_per_worker = ceil(total_items / worker_count)
          
      166. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    166. 
            

            
              
            

                    # 2. For worker i (0-indexed):
          
      167. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    167. 
            

            
              
            

                    #    - start_cursor = i * items_per_worker  (0-indexed)
          
      168. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    168. 
            

            
              
            

                    #    - end_cursor = min(start_cursor + items_per_worker, total_items)  (exclusive)
          
      169. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    169. 
            

            
              
            

                    #    - batch_size = end_cursor - start_cursor
          
      170. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    170. 
            

            
              
            

                    #
          
      171. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    171. 
            

            
              
            

                    # Example: 1000 items, 3 workers
          
      172. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    172. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   - items_per_worker = ceil(1000/3) = 334
          
      173. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    173. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   - Worker 0: start=0, end=334, size=334
          
      174. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    174. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   - Worker 1: start=334, end=668, size=334
          
      175. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    175. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   - Worker 2: start=668, end=1000, size=332
          
      176. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    176. 
            

            
              
            

                    #
          
      177. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    177. 
            

            
              
            

                    # @param total_items [Integer] Total number of items to process
          
      178. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    178. 
            

            
              
            

                    # @param worker_count [Integer] Number of workers to create configs for (must be > 0)
          
      179. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    179. 
            

            
              
            

                    # @yield [config, index] Optional block to customize each config
          
      180. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    180. 
            

            
              
            

                    # @yieldparam config [Hash] Cursor config being created
          
      181. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    181. 
            

            
              
            

                    # @yieldparam index [Integer] Worker index (0-based)
          
      182. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    182. 
            

            
              
            

                    # @return [Array<Hash>] Array of cursor configurations
          
      183. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    183. 
            

            
              
            

                    #
          
      184. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    184. 
            

            
              
            

                    # @example Basic usage (numeric cursors)
          
      185. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    185. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   configs = create_cursor_configs(1000, 5)
          
      186. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    186. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   # => [
          
      187. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    187. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   #   { 'batch_id' => '001', 'start_cursor' => 0, 'end_cursor' => 200, 'batch_size' => 200 },
          
      188. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    188. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   #   { 'batch_id' => '002', 'start_cursor' => 200, 'end_cursor' => 400, 'batch_size' => 200 },
          
      189. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    189. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   #   ...
          
      190. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    190. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   # ]
          
      191. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    191. 
            

            
              
            

                    #
          
      192. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    192. 
            

            
              
            

                    # @example With metadata customization
          
      193. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    193. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   configs = create_cursor_configs(1000, 5) do |config, i|
          
      194. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    194. 
            

            
              
            

                    #     config['worker_name'] = "worker_#{i + 1}"
          
      195. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    195. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   end
          
      196. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    196. 
            

            
              
            

                    #
          
      197. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    197. 
            

            
              
            

                    # @example Alphanumeric cursors (alphabetical ranges)
          
      198. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    198. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   alphabet_ranges = [['A', 'F'], ['G', 'M'], ['N', 'S'], ['T', 'Z']]
          
      199. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    199. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   configs = create_cursor_configs(alphabet_ranges.size, alphabet_ranges.size) do |config, i|
          
      200. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    200. 
            

            
              
            

                    #     config['start_cursor'] = alphabet_ranges[i][0]
          
      201. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    201. 
            

            
              
            

                    #     config['end_cursor'] = alphabet_ranges[i][1]
          
      202. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    202. 
            

            
              
            

                    #     config.delete('batch_size')
          
      203. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    203. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   end
          
      204. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    204. 
            
              1
            

            
              
            

                    def create_cursor_configs(total_items, worker_count)
          
      205. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    205. 
            

            
              
                
                  then: 0
                
              
                
                  else: 0
                
              
            

                      raise ArgumentError, 'worker_count must be > 0' if worker_count <= 0
          
      206. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    206. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      207. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    207. 
            

            
              
            

                      items_per_worker = (total_items.to_f / worker_count).ceil
          
      208. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    208. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      209. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    209. 
            

            
              
            

                      (0...worker_count).map do |i|
          
      210. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    210. 
            

            
              
            

                        # TAS-112: 0-indexed cursors (BREAKING CHANGE from 1-indexed)
          
      211. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    211. 
            

            
              
            

                        start_position = i * items_per_worker
          
      212. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    212. 
            

            
              
            

                        end_position = [start_position + items_per_worker, total_items].min
          
      213. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    213. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      214. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    214. 
            

            
              
            

                        config = {
          
      215. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    215. 
            

            
              
            

                          'batch_id' => format('%03d', i + 1),
          
      216. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    216. 
            

            
              
            

                          'start_cursor' => start_position,
          
      217. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    217. 
            

            
              
            

                          'end_cursor' => end_position,
          
      218. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    218. 
            

            
              
            

                          'batch_size' => end_position - start_position
          
      219. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    219. 
            

            
              
            

                        }
          
      220. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    220. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      221. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    221. 
            

            
              
            

                        # Allow customization via block
          
      222. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    222. 
            

            
              
                
                  then: 0
                
              
                
                  else: 0
                
              
            

                        yield(config, i) if block_given?
          
      223. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    223. 
            

            
              
            

                        config
          
      224. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    224. 
            

            
              
            

                      end
          
      225. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    225. 
            

            
              
            

                    end
          
      226. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    226. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      227. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    227.

    241.

      242. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    242. 
            
              1
            

            
              
            

                    def no_batches_outcome(reason:, metadata: {})
          
      243. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    243. 
            

            
              
            

                      outcome = TaskerCore::Types::BatchProcessingOutcome.no_batches
          
      244. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    244. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      245. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    245. 
            

            
              
            

                      success(
          
      246. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    246. 
            

            
              
            

                        result: {
          
      247.

                          'batch_processing_outcome' => outcome.to_h,
          
      248.

      249. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    249. 
            

            
              
            

                        }.merge(metadata)
          
      250. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    250. 
            

            
              
            

                      )
          
      251. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    251.

      252.

    253. 
            

            
              
            

                    # Create CreateBatches outcome for analyzer steps
          
      254.

      255. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    255. 
            

            
              
            

                    # @param worker_template_name [String] Name of worker template to use
          
      256. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    256. 
            

            
              
            

                    # @param cursor_configs [Array<Hash>] Array of cursor configurations
          
      257.

                    # @param total_items [Integer] Total number of items to process
          
      258.

      259.

                    # @return [StepHandlerCallResult::Success] Success result with CreateBatches outcome
          
      260. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    260.

                    # @example
          
      262.

                    #   cursor_configs = create_cursor_configs(1000, 5)
          
      263. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    263. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   return create_batches_outcome(
          
      264. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    264.

                    # TAS-125: Yield checkpoint for batch processing
          
      334. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    334. 
            

            
              
            

                    #
          
      335. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    335. 
            

            
              
            

                    # Use this method when your handler needs to persist progress and be
          
      336. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    336. 
            

            
              
            

                    # re-dispatched for continued processing. This is useful for:
          
      337. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    337. 
            

            
              
            

                    # - Processing very large datasets that exceed memory limits
          
      338. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    338. 
            

            
              
            

                    # - Providing progress visibility for long-running batch jobs
          
      339. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    339. 
            

            
              
            

                    # - Enabling graceful shutdown with resumption capability
          
      340. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    340. 
            

            
              
            

                    #
          
      341. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    341. 
            

            
              
            

                    # Unlike batch_worker_success, this does NOT complete the step.
          
      342. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    342. 
            

            
              
            

                    # Instead, it persists the checkpoint and causes the step to be
          
      343. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    343. 
            

            
              
            

                    # re-dispatched with the updated checkpoint context.
          
      344. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    344. 
            

            
              
            

                    #
          
      345. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    345. 
            

            
              
            

                    # @param cursor [Integer, String, Hash] Position to resume from
          
      346. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    346. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   - Integer: For offset-based pagination (row number)
          
      347. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    347. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   - String: For cursor-based pagination (opaque token)
          
      348. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    348. 
            

            
              
            

                    #   - Hash: For complex cursors (e.g., { page_token: "..." })
          
      349. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    349. 
            

            
              
            

                    # @param items_processed [Integer] Total items processed so far (cumulative)
          
      350. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    350.

    352. 
            

            
              
            

                    #
          
      353. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    353.

    354.

                  # Initialize a new task with workflow steps
          
      83. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    83. 
            

            
              
            

                  #
          
      84. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    84. 
            

            
              
            

                  # In the pgmq architecture, this sends a task request message to the orchestration
          
      85. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    85. 
            

            
              
            

                  # core monitored task_requests_queue, which will be processed by the Rust orchestrator
          
      86. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    86. 
            

            
              
            

                  # to create the task record and enqueue initial steps.
          
      87. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    87. 
            

            
              
            

                  #
          
      88. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    88. 
            

            
              
            

                  # @param task_request [Hash] Task initialization data
          
      89. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    89. 
            

            
              
            

                  # @return [void] No return value - operation is async via pgmq
          
      90. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    90. 
            
              1
            

            
              
            

                  def initialize_task(task_request)
          
      91. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    91. 
            

            
              
            

                    logger.info '🚀 Initializing task with pgmq architecture'
          
      92. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    92. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      93. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    93. 
            

            
              
            

                    task_request = TaskerCore::Types::TaskTypes::TaskRequest.from_hash(task_request)
          
      94. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    94. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      95. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    95. 
            

            
              
            

                    # Prepare task request message for pgmq
          
      96. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    96. 
            

            
              
            

                    task_request_message = {
          
      97. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    97. 
            

            
              
            

                      message_type: 'task_request',
          
      98. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    98. 
            

            
              
            

                      task_request: task_request.to_ffi_hash,
          
      99. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    99. 
            

            
              
            

                      enqueued_at: Time.now.utc.iso8601,
          
      100. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    100. 
            

            
              
            

                      message_id: SecureRandom.uuid
          
      101. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    101. 
            

            
              
            

                    }
          
      102. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    102. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      103. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    103. 
            

            
              
            

                    # Send message to task_requests_queue for orchestration core processing
          
      104. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    104. 
            

            
              
            

                    begin
          
      105. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    105. 
            

            
              
            

                      pgmq_client.send_message('task_requests_queue', task_request_message)
          
      106. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    106. 
            

            
              
            

                      logger.info "✅ Task request sent to orchestration queue: #{task_request.namespace}/#{task_request.name}"
          
      107. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    107. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      108. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    108. 
            

            
              
            

                      # Return void - this is now an async operation
          
      109. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    109. 
            

            
              
            

                      nil
          
      110. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    110. 
            

            
              
            

                    rescue StandardError => e
          
      111. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    111. 
            

            
              
            

                      logger.error "❌ Failed to send task request to orchestration queue: #{e.message}"
          
      112. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    112. 
            

            
              
            

                      raise TaskerCore::Errors::OrchestrationError, "Failed to send task request: #{e.message}"
          
      113. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    113. 
            

            
              
            

                    end
          
      114. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    114. 
            

            
              
            

                  rescue TaskerCore::Errors::ValidationError => e
          
      115. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    115. 
            

            
              
            

                    logger.error "❌ Validation error initializing task: #{e.message}"
          
      116. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    116. 
            

            
              
            

                    raise e
          
      117. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    117. 
            

            
              
            

                  rescue StandardError => e
          
      118. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    118.

    119. 
            

            
              
            

                    raise TaskerCore::Errors::OrchestrationError, "Task initialization failed: #{e.message}"
          
      120.

      121. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    121. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      122. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    122.

      124.

      129.

                    false

    132. 
            

            
              
            

                  # Get status information for this task handler

      134. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    134. 
            
              1
            

            
              
            

                  def status
          
      135. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    135. 
            

            
              
            

                    mode = orchestration_mode
          
      136. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    136.

                    pgmq_available = begin
          
      139.

                      !pgmq_client.nil?

                      false

    143.

      144. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    144. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      145. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    145. 
            

            
              
            

                    status_info = {
          
      146. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    146. 
            

            
              
            

                      handler_type: 'TaskHandler::Base',
          
      147. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    147. 
            

            
              
            

                      architecture: 'pgmq',
          
      148.

                      orchestration_ready: orchestration_ready?,
          
      150.

                      pgmq_available: pgmq_available,

                      task_config_loaded: !task_config.empty?,
          
      152. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    152. 
            

            
              
            

                      checked_at: Time.now.utc.iso8601
          
      153. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    153. 
            

            
              
            

                    }
          
      154. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    154. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      155. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    155. 
            

            
              
            

                    # Include embedded orchestrator status only in embedded mode
          
      156. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    156. 
            

            
              
                
                  then: 0
                
              
                
                  else: 0
                
              
            

                    status_info[:embedded_orchestrator] = embedded_orchestrator_status if mode == 'embedded'
          
      157. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    157. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      158.

                    status_info

      160. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    160. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      161. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    161.

                  def embedded_orchestrator_status

                      namespaces: orchestrator.namespaces,
          
      177.

                  then: 0

                    { running: false, error: e.message }
          
      182. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    182.

      183. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    183. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      184. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    184. 
            

            
              
            

                  # Determine orchestration mode from configuration

                  def orchestration_mode

    189. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      190. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    190. 
            

            
              
            

                    # Default to embedded mode if not specified or in test environment
          
      191. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    191. 
            

            
              
                
                  then: 0
                
              
            

                    if mode.nil?
          
      192. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    192. 
            

            
              
                
                  then: 0
                
              
            

                      if config.test_environment?
          
      193. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    193. 
            

            
              
            

                        'embedded'
          
      194. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    194. 
            

            
              
                
                  else: 0

    195.

      196. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    196. 
            

            
              
            

                      end
          
      197. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    197. 
            

            
              
                
                  else: 0
                
              
            

                    else
          
      198. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    198.

    199.

    200. 
            

            
              
            

                  rescue StandardError => e

    201.

      202. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    202. 
            

            
              
            

                    'distributed'
          
      203. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    203.

    204. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      205. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    205. 
            

            
              
            

                  # Handle task processing in embedded mode using FFI orchestrator

                  # @return [Hash] Result of step enqueueing operation
          
      208. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    208. 
            
              1
            

            
              
            

                  def handle_embedded_mode(task_uuid)
          
      209. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    209. 
            

            
              
            

                    # Use embedded orchestrator to enqueue ready steps for the task
          
      210. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    210. 
            

            
              
            

                    orchestrator = TaskerCore.embedded_orchestrator
          
      211. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    211. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      212. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    212. 
            

            
              
                
                  else: 0
                
              
                
                  then: 0
                
              
            

                    unless orchestrator.running?
          
      213. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    213. 
            

            
              
            

                      raise TaskerCore::Errors::OrchestrationError,
          
      214. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    214. 
            

            
              
            

                            'Embedded orchestration system not running. Call TaskerCore.start_embedded_orchestration! first.'
          
      215. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    215. 
            

            
              
            

                    end
          
      216. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    216. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      217. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    217. 
            

            
              
            

                    # Enqueue steps for the task - this will publish step messages to appropriate queues
          
      218. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    218. 
            

            
              
            

                    result = orchestrator.enqueue_steps(task_uuid)
          
      219. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    219. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      220. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    220.

      221. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    221. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      222. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    222. 
            

            
              
            

                    {
          
      223. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    223. 
            

            
              
            

                      success: true,
          
      224. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    224. 
            

            
              
            

                      task_uuid: task_uuid,
          
      225. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    225. 
            

            
              
            

                      message: result,
          
      226. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    226. 
            

            
              
            

                      mode: 'embedded',
          
      227. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    227. 
            

            
              
            

                      architecture: 'pgmq',
          
      228. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    228.

      229.

    231. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      232. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    232. 
            

            
              
            

                  # Handle task processing in distributed mode using pure pgmq

    233. 
            

            
              
            

                  # @param task_uuid [Integer] ID of the task to process
          
      234.

      235. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    235. 
            
              1
            

            
              
            

                  def handle_distributed_mode(task_uuid)
          
      236. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    236. 
            

            
              
            

                    # In distributed mode, we don't directly enqueue steps via FFI
          
      237. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    237. 
            

            
              
            

                    # Instead, we could publish a task processing request to a queue
          
      238. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    238. 
            

            
              
            

                    # For now, return a message indicating distributed mode handling
          
      239. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    239. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      240. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    240. 
            

            
              
            

                    logger.info "✅ Task #{task_uuid} queued for distributed processing"
          
      241. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    241. 
            

            
              
            

            
          
      242. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    242. 
            

            
              
            

                    {
          
      243. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    243. 
            

            
              
            

                      success: true,
          
      244. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    244. 
            

            
              
            

                      task_uuid: task_uuid,
          
      245. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    245. 
            

            
              
            

                      message: 'Task queued for distributed orchestration processing',
          
      246. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    246. 
            

            
              
            

                      mode: 'distributed',
          
      247. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    247. 
            

            
              
            

                      architecture: 'pgmq',
          
      248. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    248. 
            

            
              
            

                      processed_at: Time.now.utc.iso8601,
          
      249. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    249. 
            

            
              
            

                      note: 'Phase 4.5 will complete distributed orchestration integration'
          
      250. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    250. 
            

            
              
            

                    }
          
      251. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    251. 
            

            
              
            

                  end
          
      252. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    252. 
            

            
              
            

                end
          
      253. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    253. 
            

            
              
            

              end
          
      254.

                # EventPoller polls for step execution events from Rust via FFI
          
      6. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    6. 
            

            
              
            

                #
          
      7. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    7. 
            

            
              
            

                # Solves the cross-thread communication issue between Rust and Ruby by
          
      8. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    8. 
            

            
              
            

                # actively polling the Rust worker for pending events. Events are forwarded
          
      9. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    9. 
            

            
              
            

                # to EventBridge for normal processing.
          
      10. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    10. 
            

            
              
            

                #
          
      11. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    11. 
            

            
              
            

                # The poller runs in a dedicated background thread, continuously checking
          
      12. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    12. 
            

            
              
            

                # for new events from the Rust worker at a configurable interval (default 10ms).
          
      13. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    13. 
            

            
              
            

                # This approach allows Ruby to control the thread context and safely process
          
      14. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    14. 
            

            
              
            

                # events without running into Ruby's Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) limitations.
          
      15. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    15. 
            

            
              
            

                #
          
      16. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    16.

    17.

    18. 
            

            
              
            

                #   poller.start!
          
      19. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    19. 
            

            
              
            

                #   # => Spawns polling thread with 10ms interval
          
      20. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    20. 
            

            
              
            

                #
          
      21. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    21. 
            

            
              
            

                #   # Check if poller started successfully
          
      22. 
        
      
      
        
      
          
    22. 
            

            
              
            

                #   if poller.active?
          
      23.

                # @example Stopping the poller
